old_claim.note: Roads provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: Roads can improve campaign movement and supply, but they are not part of the close-order rank, shield, and spear mechanics that define the hoplite phalanx.

source_shape.source_locator: Pages 1-2: defines hoplite formation through adjacent soldiers, mutually covering shields, spears, collaboration, and training, and identifies hoplites as a Greek city-state infantry force.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The authoritative formation description identifies coordinated soldiers, shield overlap, spears, and training as the hoplite formation's operative elements.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A road network can scale marching and logistics, but those campaign functions are separable from arranging heavy infantry into a phalanx on a battlefield.

ontology_before: The target mixed campaign transport infrastructure into the direct boundary of a battlefield formation.
ontology_after: Roads remain a logistics dependency elsewhere while the hoplite node models formation-specific capabilities.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-organized-military-formations-hoplite-boundary: roads is absent.
would_reject_if: Primary or authoritative evidence demonstrates that constructed roads were indispensable to forming the seventh-century Greek hoplite phalanx.
short_reason: Roads assist campaigns, not the shield-and-rank mechanics of the phalanx.
